summary: Restored src/melee/it/item2.c into the build by adding item2.c.o and removing a large matching assembly chunk from asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s. The PR specifically handled two item2.c functions that were known to break Frank by making branches out of bounds: NON_MATCHING builds used peephole-off patched C/ASM paths, while matching builds used hybrid inline ASM where necessary. The slice also includes substantial collateral decomp/header work, especially moving Mewtwo Shadow Ball code out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s into C, adding Mewtwo SpecialN prototypes/state vars/action flags, renaming several Mewtwo Shadow Ball functions, and applying broad fighter state-var/member naming cleanup.
what_changed: Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/item2.c.o to obj_files.mk, placing item2.c back into the text object list after item.c.o and before the remaining item asm files.
Deleted approximately 1600 lines from asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s, indicating that item2.c now covers a large range that had previously remained in assembly.
Adjusted src/melee/it/item2.c around func_8026B924 and func_8026B960 to control #pragma peephole placement across NON_MATCHING and matching paths.

Replaced func_8026B960's full inline ASM implementation with a hybrid C implementation containing small embedded __asm branches for the problematic matching path; NON_MATCHING uses normal C and literals such as -1.0f.
Replaced external float labels in item2.c with direct constants where shown: lbl_804DC688 became -1.0f, and lbl_804DC68C became 0.5f in ECB/position helper code.
Expanded func_8026B9A8 in item2.c by inlining the previous helper-like What logic into the function body, preserving item state-desc animation setup and cleanup behavior.
Moved a large Mewtwo Shadow Ball/SpecialN range out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s and into src/melee/ft/chara/ftMewtwo/ftMewtwo_SpecialN.c.

Added Mewtwo Neutral Special prototypes to ftmewtwo.h, including ftMewtwo_SpecialN_CheckShadowBallRemove, CheckShadowBallCancel, SetNULL, OnTakeDamage, OnDeath, ReleaseShadowBall, PlayChargeSFX, StartAction, air/ground anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks, and Shoot.
Added ftMewtwoSpecialN state vars with fields such as isFull, releaseLag, and chargeLevel, and added SpecialN to ftMewtwoStateVars.
Renamed Mewtwo SpecialAttrs field x2230 from an unnamed u32 to HSD_GObj* x2230_shadowHeldGObj, documenting it as the held Shadow Ball GObj.

Updated references in ftmewtwo.c from func_80146E30/func_80146ED0 to named Mewtwo SpecialN lifecycle functions, though the visible diff suggests OnDeath2 calls ftMewtwo_SpecialN_OnTakeDamage and OnTakeDamage calls ftMewtwo_SpecialN_OnDeath.
Renamed several fighter state-var union members to capitalized action names across multiple characters, such as ftNess AttackHi4/AttackLw4/SpecialN/SpecialHi/SpecialLw and Mewtwo SpecialHi.
Added HSD_BUTTON_LR as 0x80000000 in include/common_structs.h for digital L/R input.
Removed rodata/sdata fragments from Fox and Ness assembly files after equivalent data moved to C or elsewhere; reviewers specifically pointed out preserving final .4byte NULL/newline formatting.
Updated Yoshi item-kind registration from raw numeric constants to It_Kind_Yoshi_EggThrow, It_Kind_Yoshi_Star, and It_Kind_Yoshi_EggLay.
smart_moves: The PR did not force all of item2.c into idealized C when the compiler/backend could not safely match it; it used separate NON_MATCHING and matching code paths with peephole pragmas and hybrid ASM to avoid Frank branch-generation failures.

For func_8026B960, the matching path retained only the fragile branch shape in inline __asm while leaving most control flow and item-data access in C, making the function more maintainable than a full inline assembly body.
Replacing anonymous float label externs with direct constants (-1.0f and 0.5f) reduced dependence on data labels where the semantic constant was clear in the local item helper code.
Adding item2.c.o before deleting the old asm body from code_80266F3C.s kept the build mapping explicit and searchable.

Mewtwo Shadow Ball work paired C restoration with header prototypes, state-var struct fields, and cross-file callsite renames, which made the newly decompiled code easier to reuse from item/projectile code.
The broad switch to conventional Fighter* fp local names and capitalized per-action state-var members reduces friction when porting matching snippets across fighter special files.
decomp_lessons: When Frank or the compiler emits invalid/out-of-range branches for a matched function, isolate only the branch-sensitive part with inline ASM or pragmas instead of reverting the entire file to asm.
Keep #pragma peephole boundaries tight and explicit around NON_MATCHING versus matching sections; misplaced peephole directives can change codegen outside the intended workaround.
Restoring a source file often requires deleting the corresponding asm chunk and adding the source object in obj_files.mk in the correct link order.

For fighter special decomp, matching is often coupled to correct state-var union layout and field names; add minimal structs and comments before relying on offsets such as Mewtwo's x2230/x2234/x2238/x2350.
Use named item-kind enum constants instead of raw item IDs when the enum is available; it improves reviewability without necessarily hurting matching.
Converting leftover rodata/sdata from asm to C should include cleanup of now-empty labels and sections, but keep table terminators like .4byte NULL and file-final newlines tidy.
Function renaming should be propagated into headers and external asm callsites where possible; the diff shows item projectile asm calls renamed Mewtwo Shadow Ball helpers.
Large fighter struct changes may need temporary layout hacks; this PR explicitly notes zero-length array union hacks in Fighter as temporary until the larger state-var struct mess is cleaned up.
naming_conventions: Use fp for local Fighter* variables instead of fighter_data in fighter special/death/onload functions.
Use ftMewtwo_SpecialN_* prefixes for Mewtwo Shadow Ball helpers and callbacks rather than raw func_80146xxx names.
Use descriptive SpecialAttrs field names for character-specific GObj pointers, e.g. x2230_shadowHeldGObj instead of x2230.
Capitalize fighter state-var union members by action family, e.g. SpecialN, SpecialHi, AttackHi4, AttackLw4, rather than lower camel-case specialN/specialHi/attackHi4.
Prefer It_Kind_* enum names over numeric item kind constants when registering or comparing item kinds.
Action flag macros use character and move prefixes, e.g. FTMEWTWO_SPECIALN_ACTION_FLAG and FTMEWTWO_SPECIALN_COLL_FLAG.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use #ifdef NON_MATCHING to keep straightforward C where exact codegen is not required, and a separate matching path when exact branch layout is needed.
Use #pragma peephole on/off around fragile codegen regions; the PR body says peephole was disabled for patched NON_MATCHING builds and hybrid ASM was used for matching builds.

For func_8026B960, replace full inline ASM with C plus an embedded __asm block containing cmpwi, beq, bgelr, bnelr, lwz, and beqlr instructions to preserve the branch behavior that the compiler would not generate correctly.

Use register-qualified locals in fragile matching code when binding operands for inline assembly, as shown by register HSD_GObj*, register s32 itemID, register Item*, and register f32 unk_timer in func_8026B960.
When moving C into the build, delete the overlapping assembly implementation from the corresponding asm file rather than leaving duplicate symbols.
For data migration out of asm, remove obsolete rodata/sdata labels but retain valid terminators such as .4byte NULL.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested keeping a clean final .4byte NULL line after removing obsolete rodata, implying formatting/newline cleanup mattered.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested keeping .4byte NULL formatting after removing Fox data labels.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested removing the include/functions.h hunk that renamed commented-out unknown Mewtwo prototypes; likely because commented prototypes were unnecessary or should not be maintained there.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked, "This can be converted to C too, right?" about a remaining Fox assembly/data section, encouraging further C conversion rather than leaving trivial asm leftovers.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the hybrid inline ASM or peephole workaround in func_8026B960.
Find PRs that fully convert remaining Fox asm/data sections after ribbanya's review question.
Audit whether ftMewtwo_OnDeath2 and ftMewtwo_OnTakeDamage kept the intended ftMewtwo_SpecialN_OnTakeDamage/OnDeath call mapping after this PR.
Find the PR that replaces the temporary Fighter zero-length array state-var union hack with a cleaner union layout.
Search later item2.c work for more raw float label replacements and It_Kind enum migrations.
